title: Vicarious touch: overlapping neural patterns between seeing and feeling touch
description: This dataset contains whole-brain EEG recordings from participants experiencing tactile stimulation and viewing videos of touch to investigate overlapping neural representations between firsthand and vicarious touch. Using time-resolved multivariate pattern analysis, the study examines whether observed touch evokes similar neural patterns to direct tactile experience across individuals with varying susceptibility to vicarious touch sensations. The dataset includes 12 experimental runs with 1728 trials (864 tactile and 864 visual) recorded at 2048 Hz from 64-channel EEG.
methods_description: EEG data were collected using a 64-channel Active Two Biosemi system at 2048 Hz with 10-20 standard electrode placement. Participants completed 12 runs divided into four blocks, each containing 36 trials alternating between tactile stimulation to the fingers and matched visual videos of touch. Inter-trial intervals were 800 ms, with each run lasting approximately 7-8 minutes. Stimuli were presented using MATLAB and Psychtoolbox.
